entries.main_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena
entries.specific_competence: (b) Describe the concept and principles of equilibrium (moment of force, centre of gravity and types of equilibrium)
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Facilitate students through manageable groups to discuss the concept and principles of equilibrium Practical Work: Facilitate the students through hands-on activities to explore the concept and principles of equilibrium
entries.assessment: Concepts and principles of equilibrium are described correctly
resources: other relevant materials and knife edge
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena
entries.specific_competence: (c) Explain the concept and laws of friction (types of friction, laws of friction)
entries.learning_activity: Practical work: Provide students with hands-on activities that allow them to explore the concepts and laws of friction Interactive simulations: Guide students through PhET interactive simulations to demonstrate the concept and the laws of friction
entries.assessment: Concepts and laws of friction are explained clearly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena
entries.specific_competence: (d) Explain the principles of simple machines (lever, pulley, inclined plane, screw jack, wheel and axle, hydraulic press)
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on different types of simple machines available in their environment Group discussion: Guide students through manageable groups, to discuss the principles of simple machines Practical Work: Provide students with hands-on activities to explore the principles of simple machines
entries.assessment: Concepts and principles of simple machines are explained correctly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: (d) Explain the principles of simple machines (lever, pulley, inclined plane, screw jack, wheel and axle, hydraulic press)
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on different types of simple machines available in their environment Group discussion: Guide students through manageable groups, to discuss the principles of simple machines Practical Work: Provide students with hands-on activities to explore the principles of simple machines
entries.assessment: Concepts and principles of simple machines are explained correctly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena
entries.specific_competence: (a) Apply mathematical knowledge to describe various principles and physical phenomena related to waves, Newton’s laws of motion, equilibrium, friction and simple machines)
entries.learning_activity: Think-ink-pair-share: Facilitate Students through think-ink pair share strategy to apply mathematical knowledge to describe various principles and physical phenomena related to Newton’s laws of motion, equilibrium, friction force, simple machines
entries.assessment: Mathematical knowledge to describe various principles and physical phenomena are correctly applied
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
entries.specific_competence: (a) Describe types of experimental variables in physics related to motion
entries.learning_activity: Cooperative learning: Facilitate students to work in groups to describe types of experimental variables in Physics
entries.assessment: Experimental variables are described clearly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
entries.specific_competence: (b) Carry out scientific investigations related to waves, laws of motion, equilibrium, friction and simple machines
entries.learning_activity: ICT based approach: Guide students in using multimedia resources such as videos, animations, and interactive simulations to carry out scientific investigations related to waves, laws of motion, equilibrium, friction and simple machines
entries.assessment: Scientific investigations related to waves, laws of motion, equilibrium, friction and simple machines are carried out
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.learning_activity: ICT based learning: Guide students the proper use of online resources in presenting the scientific investigation report
entries.assessment: A scientific report is correctly used to communicate experimental results in Physics

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
entries.specific_competence: (a) Collect and analyse data to explain various physical parameters (Waves, Newton’ s laws of motion, equilibrium, friction and simple machines)
entries.learning_activity: Collaborative learning: Guide students to work in pairs or small groups to analyse and interpret collected physics data Multimedia approach: Guide students in utilizing technology such as computer software, online tools, and simulations to analyze collected data explaining various physical quantities
entries.assessment: Collected physical data are correctly analysed to explain various physical parameters
resources: Online resources and other relevant materials
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.2 Carry out a project in Physics
entries.specific_competence: (a) Develop and carry out a research project in Physics
entries.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide students through manageable groups to discuss various procedure for carrying out a project Digital interactive simulation: Facilitate students to use virtual labs to analyse the collected data Experimentation: Guide the students using real lab instruments to perform scientific investigations
entries.assessment: A research project in Physics is developed and carried out correctly
resources: Online resources and other relevant materials
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: (a) Explore the basic tenets of heat (measurement of temperature, thermal expansion, thermal energy, transfer of thermal energy, measurement of thermal energy, vapour and humidity in relation to air temperature )
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of heat Experimentation: Guide students in manageable groups to investigate melting and boiling point of different liquids Demostrations: Guide students to show the expansion of solid and transfer of heat ICT based learning: Guide students to use of interactive simulations on the concept of heat
entries.assessment: Concepts of heat are explored appropriately
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
